macro_events.event_summary: Trump-Xi summit may shift focus to Iran geopolitics, delaying expected progress on US-China tariff de-escalation and rare earth export agreements.
primary_drivers: Prolonged tariff uncertainty weighs on China-exposed importers
Continued rare earth export restrictions support domestic/non-China REE producers
Iran geopolitical focus implies tighter sanctions risk premium on oil

beneficiaries.rationale: MP Materials is the only scaled US rare earth miner/processor (Mountain Pass). Delay in any rare earth export normalization with China extends the strategic premium and offtake urgency for domestic supply.
USA Rare Earth — domestic heavy rare earth and magnet play; direct beneficiary of continued Chinese export friction and DoD-aligned reshoring narrative.
TMC the metals company — alternative critical minerals (nickel/cobalt/REE) supply story; benefits from any narrative reinforcing China supply chain risk.
Iran-focused summit raises probability of tighter sanctions enforcement on Iranian crude exports (~1.5mbpd), supporting oil prices and integrated major margins.

losers.rationale: Highest-profile China supply chain and end-market exposure; tariff de-escalation delay removes a near-term catalyst and keeps tariff cost overhang on iPhone/Mac.
Shanghai Gigafactory and China demand exposure; prolonged tariff/rare earth (magnets for motors) uncertainty weighs on cost outlook and China sales narrative.
Deere — heavy ag/industrial exposure to China retaliatory tariffs and rare earth-dependent components; continued tariff overhang pressures FY guide.
Ford — auto OEMs depend on Chinese rare earth magnets for EV motors; delayed export normalization extends supply risk and BOM cost pressure.

headlines: Iran focus at Trump-Xi summit may delay progress on tariffs, rare earths

macro_events.event_summary: Oil prices rally as U.S.-Iran fire exchange threatens a fragile ceasefire near the Strait of Hormuz, raising risk of supply disruption through the chokepoint that handles ~20% of global oil flows.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium on crude from Hormuz disruption threat
Higher realized prices for non-Middle East oil producers
Tanker rate spike from rerouting and war-risk insurance
Fuel cost passthrough hits transports and refiners' crack spreads compress on demand fears

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large U.S./Guyana/Permian upstream production; realized crude prices rise directly with Brent/WTI, and limited Iranian/Hormuz transit exposure relative to peers means upside without the disruption risk.
Pure-play Permian E&P with no international exposure; ~100% of production benefits from higher WTI with minimal geopolitical operational risk. Highest beta to crude among large caps.
Large independent E&P with U.S., Alaska, and Norway-weighted production; high leverage to oil price with no Middle East upstream exposure. Historically trades with ~0.7+ beta to WTI moves.
Frontline operates VLCC/Suezmax tankers; Hormuz tensions force rerouting around Africa, war-risk insurance premiums spike, and tanker day-rates surge — direct revenue impact within days.

Scorpio Tankers product carrier fleet benefits from refined product rerouting and ton-mile expansion when Middle East Gulf shipping is disrupted; product tanker rates historically spike on Hormuz events.
U.S. onshore (Permian) heavy operator; Buffett-backed, high oil-price torque on free cash flow. No Iran/Hormuz transit dependency.
Cheniere is the largest U.S. LNG exporter; Qatari LNG transits Hormuz, so disruption risk pushes European/Asian buyers toward U.S. cargoes and widens spot LNG spreads.
Oilfield services lever to higher oil prices driving U.S. completion activity; sustained crude above $80 typically accelerates Permian frac demand within weeks.

losers.rationale: Delta has limited fuel hedging; jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs and tracks crude with a lag. A sustained $5-10 crude move directly compresses near-term EPS estimates.
United similarly unhedged on fuel; international long-haul routes face both higher fuel and potential Middle East airspace rerouting costs (longer flight times, crew costs).
Southwest historically ran a fuel hedge book but coverage has been reduced; pure domestic carrier with thin margins is highly sensitive to jet fuel passthrough.
Carnival's bunker fuel costs surge with crude; cruise margins are already thin and the stock historically sells off sharply on oil spikes regardless of demand backdrop.
Marathon Petroleum refining margins compress when crude spikes faster than gasoline/diesel cracks can adjust, especially if demand-destruction fears emerge alongside the supply shock.
Dow Inc. uses naphtha/ethane feedstocks; a sustained crude spike raises naphtha cracker costs globally and pressures petrochemical margins. Also sensitive to global growth fears from oil shock.
FedEx fuel surcharges lag actual jet/diesel cost increases by weeks; near-term margin hit is direct, and prior oil spikes have been negative catalysts for the stock.

headlines: Oil resumes rally as U.S.-Iran fire exchange rattles fragile Hormuz ceasefire

macro_events.event_summary: Renewed U.S.-Iran military clashes are pressuring Asia-Pacific markets and elevating Middle East geopolitical risk.
primary_drivers: Middle East oil supply risk premium (Strait of Hormuz, ~20% of global oil flow)
Risk-off rotation from equities into safe havens (gold, Treasuries, USD)

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream leverage; direct beneficiary of Brent risk premium from Iran/Hormuz threat. Earnings highly sensitive to crude price.
High-beta US E&P with Permian leverage; oil price spikes from Middle East tensions historically produce outsized moves vs majors.
Prime missile defense contractor (PAC-3, THAAD) — direct munitions replenishment buyer if US engages Iranian assets or defends regional bases/Israel.
Makes Patriot interceptors, Tomahawks, AIM-120 — exact munitions consumed in any sustained US-Iran exchange; restocking orders likely.
B-21, ICBM, and missile defense exposure; broad beneficiary of elevated DoD threat environment and FY budget supplemental risk.
Pure-play Permian E&P, high oil torque; benefits from Brent/WTI premium without refining drag.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25% of opex; sustained crude spike compresses margins. Delta has limited fuel hedging vs peers.
Same fuel cost passthrough issue; international routes (incl. Gulf overflight reroutes) add cost and disruption risk.
Cruise operator — bunker fuel cost exposure plus discretionary travel demand sensitivity if conflict broadens; Mideast itineraries directly affected.
Consumer discretionary headwind from gasoline pump price passthrough cutting into household budgets — historical pattern in 2022 oil spike.
Diesel/jet fuel surcharge lag hurts near-term margins; global air freight network faces Gulf reroute costs.

headlines: Asia-Pacific markets fall as renewed U.S.-Iran clashes keep investors on edge
